etymology.description: Phonetic: 敝 (bi) + Semantic: 巾 (cloth) = silk given as a gift or sacrificed
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts silk or cloth, represented by the 巾(巾) (jīn, "cloth") component, used as a currency or gift, with the phonetic element 敝(敝) (bì, "to damage"), indicating the sound. This reflects the historical practice of presenting cloth or silk as a form of currency.
etymology.simplification: 幣(幣) was simplified to 币(幣) by removing the 巾(巾) (jīn, "cloth") radical and retaining only the phonetic component to denote currency in modern script.

examples.chinese: 人民币(人民幣)是中国的法定货币(是中國的法定貨幣)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmínbì shì Zhōngguó de fǎdìng huòbì.
examples.english: The Renminbi is the official currency of China.

examples.chinese: 他收集了很多古代的硬币(他收集了很多古代的硬幣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shōujíle hěn duō gǔdài de yìngbì.

examples.chinese: 在市场上(在市場上)，不同的货币有不同的价值(不同的貨幣有不同的價值)。
examples.pinyin: Zài shìchǎng shàng, bùtóng de huòbì yǒu bùtóng de jiàzhí.
examples.english: In the market, different currencies have different values.
